page: OVHcloud r2-15 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance configured on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) cores running at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by 15.0 GB of RAM, which provides a resource density of 7.5 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 50 GB SSD. The instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the r2-15 operates within lower-tier performance brackets. The dedicated Intel Haswell CPU achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1098.99 and a multi-core score of 2095.09. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 148.62 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 22.28 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. PassMark testing yielded a CPU score of 3050.61 and a database operations score of 1436.35, reflecting limited computational throughput. Redis SET operations reached 3.07 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput was measured at 2.73 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r2-15 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on dedicated hardware, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-frequency processing or modern instruction sets like AVX-512. However, the older Haswell architecture presents a performance tradeoff, as demonstrated by weak results in cryptography and software compilation. This instance is suited for lightweight database operations, caching layers, Redis deployments, and basic memory-intensive background tasks rather than heavy parallel computing.
description: OVHcloud r2-15 is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell) at 2.2 GHz, 15.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB SSD. The instance lacks GPU acceleration and baseline network bandwidth is limited. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1098.99 and a multi-core score of 2095.09. Memory bandwidth is also modest, with uncached large-block reads at 22.28 GB/sec. Given its high memory-to-core ratio of 7.5 GB per core, the r2-15 provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not demand high CPU throughput. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, Redis caching, and basic memory-intensive background tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud r2-15 is a memory-optimized KVM virtual server featuring 2 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs, 15 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB SSD, designed for lightweight database operations and caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated dual-core processing and balanced memory allocation for lightweight caching and database workloads.

15.0 GB of RAM providing 7.5 GB per core
Best suited for Redis caching and lightweight database operations
